name: Mars Pathfinder
first: First wheels on Mars; demonstrated airbag landing
description: The mission that proved cheap interplanetary missions could work. Pathfinder used a novel airbag landing system — bouncing dozens of times across the surface before coming to rest — and delivered Sojourner, the first rover to operate on another planet. Sojourner traversed 100 m, examined a dozen rocks, and worked far past its 7-day design lifetime. Pathfinder operated for 2 months 27 days; the mission's success revived NASA's interplanetary programme after the failures of the 1990s.

events.label: EDL
events.note: Direct atmospheric entry — no orbit insertion. Heat shield, parachute, retro-rockets, then airbag inflation.

events.label: TOUCHDOWN
events.note: Lander bounces 15 times across Ares Vallis before coming to rest.
